data.title: Fed's Goolsbee concerned about inflation in non-tariff items as CPI rises by 0.2% in July, year-over-year at 2.7%.
data.description: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee expressed concern as producer prices experienced a sudden increase, shaking the Federal Reserve's confidence. The unexpected rise in inflation was primarily seen in areas not vulnerable to tariffs. This shift raised worries as Goolsbee noted that recent inflation reports had been reassuring until now. The latest data showed a 0.2% uptick in July's Consumer Price Index (CPI), aligning with predictions and down from June's 0.3%. The year-over-year CPI remained at 2.7%, slightly lower than the anticipated 2.8%. Goolsbee emphasized the need for more positive data to assess the situation accurately. The QQQ ETF demonstrated swift movements amid these developments. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: US retail sales grew by 0.5% in July to $726.1 billion, 3.9% year-on-year. Consumer spending remains strong despite tariffs. SPY ETF at new highs.
data.description: In July, U.S. retail sales surged, showing American consumers' resilience despite higher import prices and trade tariff concerns under President Donald Trump. The SPY ETF hit new record highs, reflecting positive market sentiment. Retail and food service sales climbed by 0.5% from the prior month to $726.1 billion, up by 3.9% year-over-year. June's sales figures were revised upward from 0.6% to 0.9%, leading to a year-over-year growth of 4.4%. Key areas of increased spending included motor vehicles and parts dealers with a 1.6% monthly rise. The robust sales data aligns with economists' expectations, suggesting a healthy consumer market. For detailed information, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Zoom Communications Inc reported strong fiscal Q2 earnings with revenue up 4.7% YoY to $1.22 billion. Analysts have varying ratings and price targets, with Rosenblatt Securities highlighting the company's 11-quarter revenue growth streak. Management raised fiscal 2026 guidance for revenue, earnings, and free cash flow, reflecting top-line stability and industry-leading profitability.
data.description: Shares of Zoom Communications Inc (NASDAQ: ZM) surged after the company reported strong fiscal Q2 earnings. Rosenblatt Securities' Catharine Trebnick raised the price target to $110, highlighting 4.7% revenue growth to $1.22B and $1.53 pro forma earnings per share. KeyBanc's Jackson Ader reduced the price target to $69, and RBC's Rishi Jaluria increased it to $100, emphasizing accelerating enterprise demand. Wedbush's Daniel Ives maintained a $95 price target, and Needham's Joshua Reilly kept it at $100, citing the company's Virtual Agent 2.0 product securing significant deals. Trekbnick noted Zoom's strength in revenue growth and profitability, with raised fiscal 2026 guidance reflecting top-line stability.

data.title: Huntsman Cancer Institute orders second MEVION S250i Proton Therapy System, serving patients across 5 states. #ProtonTherapy
data.description: Mevion Medical Systems revealed today that the Huntsman Cancer Institute at the University of Utah has agreed to acquire a second MEVION S250i Proton Therapy System. This marks a significant milestone as Huntsman is the sole proton therapy center in the Mountain West region, catering to patients from Utah, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, and Wyoming. Ever since the inauguration of the Senator Orrin G. Hatch Center for Proton Therapy in 2021, the institute has delivered crucial treatments, showcasing its dedication to patient care.

data.title: Nurse invents FORTIFIED EXCEL case for telemetry monitor, based in Snellville, Ga., protects expensive equipment safely.
data.description: A resident of Snellville, Georgia and registered nurse has unveiled a new product designed to safeguard telemetry monitors. The FORTIFIED EXCEL is the result of the inventor's desire to provide enhanced protection for this crucial and costly equipment. This innovation aims to ensure the safety and reliability of telemetry monitors, addressing a critical need in the medical field.

data.title: Dow hits record high above 45,680 with a 2% surge, S&P 500 gains 1.4% on Powell's dovish message at Jackson Hole.
data.description: Wall Street experienced a significant surge on Friday following a dovish speech from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell at Jackson Hole. Powell hinted at potential interest rate cuts in response to a cooling labor market, signaling the Fed's readiness to adjust its policy stance. He also expressed a more relaxed stance on inflation risks from trade tariffs, viewing them as likely to cause temporary price increases rather than sustained inflation. The rally saw all sectors in the S&P 500 ending in positive territory, with consumer discretionary, real estate, industrials, and materials each posting over 2% gains. The Dow Jones Industrial Average jumped 2%, hitting new all-time highs above 45,680, while the S&P 500 rose by 1.4%. These developments can be further explored on Benzinga.com for a detailed analysis.
